overview: Dimir Murktide is a tempo-control deck that uses discard and counterspells to disrupt the opponent while building a graveyard advantage to cast Murktide Necromancer and Flow State for explosive, game-winning turns. It excels against combo and midrange decks by out-valueing them with cheap threats and graveyard recursion.
win_conditions: Flow State casting multiple cheap threats (Orcish Bowmasters, Psychic Frog, Thought Scour, counters) followed by Murktide Necromancer to recur them.
Alternatively, Murktide + graveyard shenanigans to overwhelm the board.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches), at least one early interaction (Thoughtseize, Inquisition of the Deep, Daze, Force of Negation), and a Flow State or cheap threat (Psychic Frog, Orcish Bowmasters, Thought Scour).
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan pure land, three-plus lands without interaction, or hands lacking a turn-1 play.

early_game: Turn 1: Cast Thoughtseize/Inquisition on key threats (Amulet, Energy, Storm combo pieces, Affinity artifacts).
Turn 2: Play a shock land, fetch a basic, cast Daze/Force of Negation/Thought Scour, or drop Psychic Frog/Orcish Bowmasters.
Prioritize fetching duals for shock lands to preserve life total against aggro.
mid_game: Use Thought Scour to fuel Murktide and Flow State while digging for answers.
don't waste them on non-essential spells.
Keep graveyard size in check against hatebear decks, but maintain it for Murktide recursion.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when you can swing with Flow State.
Hold back on casting expensive spells if you lack counters/discard to protect them.
Use counters to protect your Flow State or key threats.
late_game: Flow State casting multiple cheap threats (Orcish Bowmasters, Psychic Frog, Thought Scour, counters) followed by Murktide Necromancer to recur them.
Alternatively, Murktide + graveyard shenanigans to overwhelm the board.
Go all-in when you have 3+ spells/threats in hand/graveyard, enough mana to cast Flow State, and a clear path to victory (opponent low on life, no immediate removal).
Don't commit if you're facing graveyard hate or have no way to protect your combo.

sequencing_tips: Flow State is your win condition, not just a tempo play.
Sequence it carefully: cast cheap threats first, then counters/discard, then Flow State to cast them all for free.
Don't cast Flow State on turn 2 unless you have a clear path to lethal.
Murktide requires a graveyard, so be mindful of Rest in Peace or Surgical Extraction.
Thought Scour is your best friend for fueling both Murktide and Flow State.

matchups.advice: Use Thoughtseize to remove counterspells early. Cast Flow State or threats in response to counterspells to break the stack. Use Daze/Force of Negation to protect your own spells.
Out-value them with cheap threats and counters. Thoughtseize to remove key ramp pieces. Flow State to swing before they can cast their big threats.
Stabilize with early counters and discard. Use Psychic Frog/Orcish Bowmasters to trade efficiently. Flow State to swing for lethal or to clear the board. Don't overextend; play around removal.

Pressure with cheap threats and discard. Use Flow State to out-value their removal. Dig for answers with Thought Scour/Brainstorm. Force them to use resources on your threats while you set up Murktide.

Win the resource war. Prioritize Thoughtseize/Inquisition to remove their counters and threats. Use counters to protect your Flow State. Graveyard hate (Rest in Peace) can shut down their recursion. Out-value them with better sequencing and card advantage.

common_threats: Watch for combo win conditions (Amulet Titan, Ruby Storm, Energy) and artifact acceleration (Affinity).
Note when opponents tap out for big threats, as you can counter or discard them.
Track graveyard size to anticipate Murktide or opponent recursion.
